summary: Decompiled the MSL string library object by replacing asm/MSL/string.o with src/MSL/string.o while preserving a matching DOL. The new C file implements __StringRead, strchr, strncmp, strcmp, strncpy, strcpy, and strlen, and downstream assembly callsites were relabeled from anonymous func_80325xxx names to standard C library symbol names.
what_changed: Added src/MSL/string.c with 329 lines of C replacing the previous hand-assembled MSL string implementation.
Deleted the function bodies from asm/MSL/string.s for the decompiled routines, leaving the object to be built from C via obj_files.mk.
Added prototypes in include/functions.h for __StringRead, strchr, strncmp, strncpy, strcpy, and strlen, replacing several unknown func_80325xxx declarations.
Renamed assembly callsites from func_80325878 to strchr, func_80325B04 to strlen, func_80325A50 to strcpy, func_80325A0C to strncpy, and lbl_803257E8 to __StringRead.
Updated many existing asm files only at symbol-reference level, including printf.s, strtoul.s, CARDCreate.s, CARDRename.s, mcc/fio.s, mcc.s, and multiple melee/sysdolphin asm files.
smart_moves: Kept the C implementation close to the original optimized MSL behavior instead of writing naive libc routines, including word-aligned fast paths for strcmp and strcpy.
Preserved the zero-byte detection constants as static sdata variables lbl_804D5B58 = 0x80808080 and lbl_804D5B5C = 0xfefefeff, matching the original code generation and data references.
Used standard library names for recognized routines, making cross-object callsites more readable without changing call structure.
Implemented __StringRead with the same get, unget, and error-test action model used by strtoul, allowing strtoul.s to reference a named callback instead of lbl_803257E8.
Submitted with multiple decomp.me scratch links and stated that the PR produces a matching DOL, giving reviewers reproducibility evidence.
decomp_lessons: For SDK/MSL library code, matching may require reconstructing vendor-optimized algorithms, not just semantic equivalents.
String routines may use pointer pre-decrement patterns, word alignment checks, and magic constants for zero-byte detection; preserving these shapes can be necessary for matching PowerPC output.
When a library object is decompiled, update all existing asm callsites to the canonical symbol names so later decompilation work inherits better references.
Switching an object from asm to src requires both removing/reducing the asm body and updating obj_files.mk; doing only one side is not enough.
Header prototypes should be introduced as soon as formerly anonymous library routines are identified, because many unrelated systems call libc/MSL functions.

Parenthesized function definitions such as char * (strcpy)(...) and unsigned long (strlen)(...) can be used in libc-style code to avoid macro-name interference while still emitting the expected symbol.
naming_conventions: Use standard C library names for recognized MSL routines: strchr, strncmp, strcmp, strncpy, strcpy, strlen.
Use double-underscore internal names for MSL helper callbacks, as with __StringRead.
Keep original lbl_804D... names for static data when the underlying semantic name is not established or when exact small-data layout matters.
Replace func_80325xxx placeholders in asm callsites with canonical names once the function identity is known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced branch-and-link targets in asm files with newly identified symbols while preserving the same addresses and branch offsets.
Matched strcmp and strcpy using same-boundary alignment checks before word-at-a-time loops, falling back to bytecopy labels for unaligned or terminating-byte cases.
Used MSL zero-byte detection logic based on 0xfefefeff and 0x80808080 constants rather than per-byte tests in optimized paths.
Kept __StringRead action dispatch compatible with callers that pass action values for get, unget, and test-for-error.
Used #pragma ANSI_strict off/on around the C implementation, suggesting compiler-dialect control was part of the match.
review_feedback: One collaborator review approved the PR with no written review body.
No issue comments or review comments were present in the slice, so there is no detailed reviewer rationale to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that move additional MSL libc routines from asm/MSL to src/MSL.
Search for remaining func_80325xxx or lbl_803257E8 references after this PR.
Compare later header cleanup for const-correct prototypes of strcmp, strchr, strcpy, strncpy, and strlen.
Look for reuse of the 0x80808080/0xfefefeff zero-byte detection constants in other MSL decompilations.
